reflection:
That thought record you did yesterday, intensity dropping from a 6 to a 1, that's actually real progress. The situation was genuinely stressful (rushing to Renata, laptop on your lap, doing five things at once) and you still landed on "don't beat yourself" and it stuck. That's not a small thing.

The screens though. You narrated it yourself in your sleep notes. Movie at 7, laptop until 10, Duolingo until nearly midnight, then six hours, restless legs, awake at 6. You *knew* it was a trap while you were in it. The pattern isn't that you're unaware, it's that evenings pull you in anyway. The question worth sitting with is what you're avoiding or chasing in those hours, because it keeps happening.

Mood and energy both at 7 yesterday, best you've logged in four days. The day itself was full and good. Filip and Nasti being excited about the brokerage thing, the session with Renata, the content meeting. Something is gaining momentum even while sleep is still fighting you.

The chest tightness and itching, trust your instinct there. Avocado is high histamine and you've been eating it alongside bread for three days. You took Telfast. Keep watching it and pull back on both if it shows up again today.

One thing for today: screens off at 9:30. Not 10. Not "almost". 9:30. You already know what late screens cost you.
opener: CBT intensity 6→1 yesterday. Real work. But screens are still the trap.
focus_for_today: Screens off at 9:30pm, hard stop. You already know why.
